content: Subconscious is an invisible memory manager, not an agent or daemon
Subconscious role is to make the 70B feel like it remembers everything without the user knowing Subconscious exists
Previous architecture was bottom-up (what to put on the compute lane), Subconscious is top-down (what does the user experience)
The user never sees or interacts with Subconscious directly
Subconscious memory lifecycle has four phases: Ingestion, Storage, Retrieval, Maintenance
Ingestion phase extracts atomic facts from every 70B exchange
Storage uses structured memory store with content, timestamp, source, entity links, relevance decay, access count, and embedding
Retrieval answers what memories matter given the users current message before every 70B generation
Maintenance includes consolidation, contradiction resolution, decay, and strengthening of memories
Subconscious gets smarter through three mechanisms: memory store growth, relevance score adaptation via usage statistics, and extraction skill improvement via 70B feedback
Relevance scores adapt through usage statistics without any model weight changes
Extraction skill can improve via 70B feedback during idle time
Subconscious is fundamentally different from an agent daemon: an agent does tasks, Subconscious manages memory
The 70B should surface things the user forgot they mentioned and never surface irrelevant noise
Memory extraction is structured extraction of atomic facts, not summarization
